calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Hurlburt for (administrator) Maria Isabel Palacpac-Luga.

The Court stated that it questions the $435.00 and asked if counsel had any argument. Mr. Hurlburt stated that he will pay it. The Court asked if counsel wishes to file a declaration regarding reimbursement of costs. Mr. Hurlburt stated that he filed a response to the examiner's notes from 9/6/16 and that it covers all of the information. The Court asked about the agreement and how to square it with the heirs. Mr. Hurlburt stated that Ms. Palacpac died in 1979, that he has a declaration from himself as a real estate agent stating the value, that the statute allows for gains, that he asks the court to use discretion, that the fees should be based on today's appraisal, that the beneficiaries who have counsel have stipulated to it, that the examiner's notes indicate that some of the fees are not extraordinary, that he assumes the examiner is correct and that it brings the fees to $16,782.00. The Court took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-13-2016 Continued To Oct-24-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-30-2016 Continued To Sep-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-01-2016 Off Calendar

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-29-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Benjamin Humphreys for petitioner Maria Palacpac-Laga; John Boessenecker for Ehlane Palacpac

Mr. Humphreys stated that a partial rather than final distribution was appropriate and beneficial, as it would permit a step-up in basis in vauluation of the interests of the property, and would allow the sale of the property to be by the administrator of the estate rather than by an owner, thus allowing for a sale "as-is" without application of disclosure requirements that would arise in a sale by owner situation. Mr. Boessenecker stated that the Estate of Leonida Palapac is not ready to be closed, and that the preliminary distribution should go forward as provided in the parties' settlement agreement. He added that his client would save expenditure of over two hundred thousand dollars in capital gains taxes if the preliminary distribution is approved as requested in the petition.
The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not reported)

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar May-23-2016 Continued To Jun-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
